setup.title: I. Setup & General Rules
setup.q1: How many cards are dealt to each player?
setup.a1: At the beginning of each Age, each player is dealt a hand of 7 cards. This applies to Age I, II, and III.
setup.q2: How many Guild (Purple) cards do we use in Age III?
setup.a2_p1: You must create a deck of Guilds equal to the number of players + 2.
setup.a2_p2: The remaining Guilds are returned to the box without being looked at.
setup.q3: How much money does each player start with?
setup.a3: Each player begins the game with 3 coins of value "1".
setup.q4: What happens if I make a mistake and build an illegal structure (e.g., I couldn't afford it)?
setup.a4: If a player builds a card they do not have the resources for, or builds a duplicate structure, the card is discarded, and the player gains 3 coins instead (treating it as a discard action).
setup.q5: In a tie for victory points, who wins?
setup.a5: The tie-breaker is the player with the most coins in their treasury. If there is still a tie, there is no further tie-breaker (players share the victory).

gameplay.title: II. Card Construction & Gameplay
gameplay.q1: Can I build two of the same card (e.g., two 'Scriptorium' cards)?
gameplay.a1_p1: No. A player can never build two identical structures (cards with the same name and illustration) in your city.
gameplay.a1_p2: Note: You can build different cards that provide the same benefit (e.g., two different cards that both provide stone).
gameplay.q2: How do I build a card for free (Chains)?
gameplay.a2_p1: Some structures in Age II and III have the name of a structure from the previous Age listed near the cost. If you have the required previous structure built in your city, you may build the new card for free (without paying resource or coin costs).
gameplay.a2_p2: Clarification: You do not need the Age I precursor to build the Age III card if you have the Age II precursor. The chain works directly from the previous age to the current one.
gameplay.q3: Can I discard a card for coins even if I have the resources to build it?
gameplay.a3: Yes. You may choose to discard a card to gain 3 coins regardless of whether you could have built it. You do not need to show the card to opponents; it is placed face-down in the discard pile.
gameplay.q4: Can I look through the discard pile?
gameplay.a4: No. Players cannot look through the discard pile at any time, unless they are using the specific ability of the Halicarnassus Wonder.
gameplay.q5: Do I have to show my card to everyone when I play it?
gameplay.a5: Yes. All players reveal their selected cards simultaneously.
gameplay.q6: What happens to the 7th card in my hand at the end of an Age?
gameplay.a6_p1: It is discarded (face-down) and you do not receive 3 coins for it.
gameplay.a6_p2: Exception: The Babylon Wonder (Side B, Stage 2) allows the player to play this 7th card instead of discarding it.

commerce.title: III. Commerce & Resources
commerce.q1: Do I lose my resources when I use them to build?
commerce.a1: No. Resources produced by your city are never 'spent' or lost. They renew every turn and can be used once per turn.
commerce.q2: If I sell a resource to a neighbor, can I still use it myself?
commerce.a2: Yes. Selling a resource only means your neighbor pays you coins to use a 'copy' of that resource for their turn. You retain full use of your own resource for your own construction during that same turn.
commerce.q3: Can I refuse to sell resources to a neighbor?
commerce.a3: No. If you produce a resource (via Brown/Gray cards or your Wonder board initial resource), your neighbors may buy it. You cannot block them.
commerce.q4: Can I buy resources from a neighbor's Commercial (Yellow) cards?
commerce.a4_p1: Generally No. You can typically only buy resources produced by Brown (Raw Materials), Gray (Manufactured Goods) cards, and the Wonder Board's starting resource.
commerce.a4_p2: Note: Some yellow cards (like the Forum or Caravansary) produce resources, but these specifically state that these resources cannot be purchased by neighbors.
commerce.q5: Can I buy resources from the neighbor of my neighbor?
commerce.a5: No. You may only trade with the two cities immediately to your left and right.
commerce.q6: The rulebook says 'each resource symbol can only be bought once per turn.' What does this mean?
commerce.a6_p1: It means you cannot buy the same specific symbol twice.
commerce.a6_li1: Example: If your neighbor has a Stone Pit (1 Wood), you can buy that 1 Wood. You cannot pay 4 coins to buy that single Wood twice.
commerce.a6_li2: Example: If your neighbor has a Tree Farm (1 Wood) and a Lumber Yard (1 Wood), they have two Wood symbols. You may buy both (paying 2 coins for each).
commerce.q7: Can I buy resources that my neighbor just played this turn?
commerce.a7: No. You can only buy resources available at the start of the turn. Cards played simultaneously in the current turn are not available for trade until the next turn.

wonders.title: IV. Wonders
wonders.q1: Do I have to build the stages of my Wonder in order?
wonders.a1: Yes. Wonder stages must be built from left to right (Stage 1, then 2, then 3).
wonders.q2: Do I have to build my Wonder to win?
wonders.a2: No. Wonder construction is optional. You can win the game without building any stages.
wonders.q3: Can I build a Wonder stage more than once?
wonders.a3: No. Each stage can be built only once.
wonders.q4: Can I use resources produced by a Wonder stage immediately?
wonders.a4: No. The resource is available starting the turn after the stage is completed.
wonders.a5_li1: Babylon (Side B): If I build the 2nd stage (allowing me to play the 7th card), do I play it immediately? Yes, playing the 7th card is considered a "7th turn." Resources reset for this extra action.
wonders.a5_li2: Olympia (Side B): If I copy a Guild, does it count as a Guild structure for scoring? No, the Wonder stage itself is not a Guild card (relevant for Leaders/expansions).
wonders.a5_li3: Halicarnassus: If I use the ability to build from the discard, can I perform a naval construction (if playing Armada)? No.

scoring.title: V. Scoring
scoring.q1: What is the official scoring order?
scoring.a2_li1: Sets of Identical Symbols: Square the number of identical symbols (e.g., 3 Gears = 3x3 = 9 points).
scoring.a2_li2: Sets of 3 Different Symbols: 7 points for every complete set of 3 different symbols (Tablet, Compass, Gear).
scoring.a2_p2: Note: One card can contribute to both calculations.
scoring.q3: Do I count Victory Points on cards (Blue/Green/Guilds) during the game?
scoring.a3: No. Victory points from structures are counted only at the very end of the game. However, Military Conflict tokens are collected at the end of each Age.
scoring.q4: Do resources on my Wonder board count toward cards that give points for 'Brown/Gray cards'?
scoring.a4: No. The Wonder board is not considered a card. If a Guild gives points for every Brown card, the Wonder's starting resource does not count.
scoring.q5: Do I get points for leftover coins?
scoring.a5: Yes. You earn 1 Victory Point for every 3 coins you have at the end of the game.

compatibility.title: VI. Edition & Compatibility
compatibility.q1: I have the 1st Edition base game. Can I use 2nd Edition expansions?
compatibility.a1_p1: No. The card backs and graphic design are different. The 2nd Edition (released 2020) is incompatible with 1st Edition expansions, and vice versa.
compatibility.a1_p2: Note: The Edifice expansion is the exception; it is compatible with both editions.
compatibility.q2: Which expansion should I buy first?
compatibility.a2_li1: Leaders is widely considered the "must-have" first expansion as it adds strategy without too much rules overhead.
compatibility.a2_li2: Cities is excellent for adding more player interaction (debt, diplomacy) and supports up to 8 players.
compatibility.a2_li3: Armada adds significant depth (naval tracks) but increases complexity and table space.

setup.title: I. Setup & General Integration
setup.q1: How much money does each player start with?
setup.a1: Each player begins the game with 6 coins (instead of the usual 3 from the base game).
setup.q2: Do we use the new Guild (Purple) cards included in the Leaders expansion?
setup.a2: Yes. You must add the 4 new Guilds included in the Leaders box to the Guilds from the base game. When creating the Age III deck, you randomly keep a number of Guilds equal to the number of players + 2 and return the rest to the box.
setup.q3: Is the "Rome" Wonder board compatible with the base game alone?
setup.a3: No. The Colosseum of Rome is specifically designed to be played with the Leaders expansion, as its effects relate directly to Leader cards (reducing their cost or drawing more of them).
setup.q4: How does the Leader draft work?
setup.a4_p1: Before Age I begins, a "Leader Phase" takes place. Each player is dealt 4 Leader cards.
setup.a4_li1: Choose 1 Leader to keep and place it face-down.
setup.a4_li2: Pass the remaining cards to the player to your right.
setup.a4_li3: Repeat this process until each player has selected 4 Leaders.

recruitment.title: II. The Recruitment Phase (Gameplay)
recruitment.q1: When do I play my Leaders?
recruitment.a1: At the beginning of each Age (I, II, and III), before the hand of Age cards is dealt, there is a specific "Recruitment Phase".
recruitment.q2: What actions can I take with a Leader card during Recruitment?
recruitment.a2_p1: You choose one Leader from your hand of 4 (or fewer in later Ages) and perform one of three actions:
recruitment.a2_li1: Recruit: Play the Leader face-up by paying its coin cost (shown in the upper left).
recruitment.a2_li2: Build a Wonder Stage: Use the Leader card face-down as a marker to build the next stage of your Wonder. You must pay the Wonder stage's resource cost, not the Leader's cost.
recruitment.a2_li3: Discard for Coins: Discard the Leader face-down to gain 3 coins from the bank.
recruitment.q3: Do I lose the Leaders I don't play?
recruitment.a3: No. You start with 4 Leaders. You play one at the start of Age I and keep the remaining 3 for the next Age. You play one at the start of Age II and keep the remaining 2. You play one at the start of Age III. The final remaining Leader is returned to the box and is not used.
recruitment.q4: Can I use a Leader to build a Wonder stage if I don't have the resources for the Wonder?
recruitment.a4: No. The Leader card acts only as a construction marker. You must still meet the resource requirements listed on your Wonder board to build the stage.

abilities.title: III. Leader Abilities & Interactions
abilities.q1: Does the "Imhotep" Leader reduce the coin cost of Wonder stages?
abilities.a1: No. Imhotep reduces the resource cost of Wonder stages by one resource of your choice (Raw Material or Manufactured Good). He does not reduce coin costs.
abilities.q2: How does "Solomon" work with the Halicarnassus Wonder?
abilities.a2: Solomon allows you to pick a card from the discard pile and build it for free. If both Solomon and the Halicarnassus Wonder ability (which also builds from the discard) trigger in the same turn, Solomon resolves first, followed by Halicarnassus.
abilities.q3: Can I combine the effect of "Hatshepsut" with Trading Posts?
abilities.a3: Yes. Hatshepsut allows you to gain 1 coin from the bank whenever you buy a resource from a neighbor. This stacks with Trading Posts (which reduce the purchase cost to 1). You effectively pay 1 coin to the neighbor and gain 1 coin from the bank.
abilities.a4: Once Bilkis is in play, you may purchase any 1 resource of your choice from the bank by paying 1 coin. This can be done once per turn.
abilities.q5: Do Scientific Leaders (e.g., Euclid, Ptolemy) count toward Green card scoring?
abilities.a5: Yes. Scientific symbols on Leaders are added to the symbols on your Green cards and Wonder board. They contribute to both sets of identical symbols and sets of 3 different symbols.
abilities.a6: Plato grants 7 Victory Points for every complete set of 7 different card colors (Brown, Gray, Blue, Red, Yellow, Green, Purple) in your city.
abilities.a7: At the end of the game, Midas grants 1 Victory Point for every 3 coins in your treasury. This is added to the standard points you get for coins (which is also 1 VP per 3 coins). Effectively, you score 2 VP for every 3 coins.
abilities.q8: Does the "Courtesan's Guild" count as a Leader?
abilities.a8: No. Although it allows you to copy a neighbor's Leader effect, the Guild card itself is not considered a "Leader card" for effects that specifically count Leaders.
abilities.q9: If I use "Hiram" (which gives points for Guilds), does the copied Guild from the Olympia Wonder (Side B) count?
abilities.a9: No. The Olympia B stage that copies a Guild is still considered a Wonder stage, not a Guild card, so Hiram does not grant points for it.

baseRules.title: IV. Base Game Rules Clarifications
baseRules.q1: Can I build two of the same card (e.g., two 'Scriptorium' cards)?
baseRules.a1: No. A player can never build two identical structures (cards with the same name) in their city.
baseRules.q2: Can I buy resources from a neighbor's Commercial (Yellow) cards?
baseRules.a2: No. You can only buy resources produced by the city's board, Brown cards (Raw Materials), and Gray cards (Manufactured Goods). Resources produced by Yellow cards (like the Forum or Caravansary) are for the owner only.
baseRules.q3: Can I refuse to sell resources to a neighbor?
baseRules.a3: No. If you have a tradeable resource (Brown/Gray/Wonder starting resource), your neighbors may buy it. You cannot block them, and their purchase does not prevent you from using the resource yourself.
baseRules.q4: Can I buy the same resource symbol twice?
baseRules.a4: The rule is "each resource symbol can only be bought once per turn." This means if a neighbor has a card with 1 Wood, you can buy that 1 Wood. You cannot pay double to buy that specific Wood symbol twice. However, if they have two separate Wood cards, you can buy one Wood from each.
baseRules.q5: In a tie for victory points, who wins?
baseRules.a5: The tie-breaker is the player with the most coins in their treasury. If there is still a tie, the victory is shared.
baseRules.q6: Do I get points for leftover coins?
baseRules.a6: Yes. You earn 1 Victory Point for every 3 coins you have at the end of the game.
baseRules.q7: What happens to the final card in my hand at the end of an Age?
baseRules.a7: It is discarded without being played, and you do not receive coins for it. Exception: The Babylon Wonder (Side B, Stage 2) allows the player to play this last card instead of discarding it.
baseRules.q8: Can I look through the discard pile?
baseRules.a8: No. Players cannot look through the discard pile unless they are using the specific ability of the Halicarnassus Wonder or the Solomon Leader.
baseRules.q9: Can I mix 1st Edition and 2nd Edition games?
baseRules.a9_p1: It is highly advised not to mix editions. The card backs and graphic design are different. You should stick to expansions that match your base game edition.

setup.title: I. Setup & General Changes
setup.q1: How does the card setup change with Cities?
setup.a1_p1: You must add City cards (Black cards) to the standard Age decks.
setup.a1_li1: Procedure: Shuffle the Black cards for each Age separately. Randomly draw a number of Black cards equal to the number of players for each Age and shuffle them into the corresponding base game Age deck.
setup.a1_li2: Note: Return unused Black cards to the box.
setup.q2: Do we use the new Guilds?
setup.a2: Yes. Shuffle the 3 new Guilds included in Cities with the original Guilds. Then, randomly select the number of players + 2.
setup.q3: How many cards are dealt to each player?
setup.a3: Each player is dealt a hand of 8 cards at the start of each Age (instead of the usual 7).
setup.a4: Yes. Since you have 8 cards, you will play 7 turns per Age.
setup.q5: Does this expansion allow for an 8th player?
setup.a5: Yes. Cities provides enough cards for 8 players.

debt.title: II. New Mechanics: Debt & Coins
debt.q1: Some cards force other players to lose coins. What happens if I don't have enough money?
debt.a1_p1: If a card effect forces you to lose coins, you must pay as much as you can. For every coin you cannot pay, you must take a Debt Token equal to the unpaid amount.
debt.a1_li1: Example: You must lose 2 coins but have 0. You take -2 points worth of Debt tokens.
debt.q2: Can I choose to keep my money and take Debt tokens instead?
debt.q3: Do Debt tokens count as negative Victory Points?
debt.a3: Yes. At the end of the game, subtract the total value of your Debt tokens from your final score.
debt.q4: Is there a limit to how much Debt I can have?
debt.q5: Do I lose coins to the player who played the card, or to the bank?
debt.a5: Coins lost via Black cards are returned to the bank.

diplomacy.title: III. New Mechanics: Diplomacy (The Dove)
diplomacy.q1: What do cards with the Dove symbol do?
diplomacy.a1: When you build a card with a Dove symbol, you immediately take a Diplomacy Token.
diplomacy.q2: When do I use the Diplomacy Token?
diplomacy.a2_p1: At the end of the current Age, during the Resolution of Military Conflicts.
diplomacy.a2_li1: You must discard one Diplomacy token to withdraw from the conflict.
diplomacy.q3: What happens to my neighbors if I use Diplomacy?
diplomacy.a3: Because you have withdrawn, your Left Neighbor and your Right Neighbor now fight each other.
diplomacy.q4: Can I choose not to use my Diplomacy token if I think I will win the war?
diplomacy.a4: No. If you have a Diplomacy token, you must use it.
diplomacy.q5: What if I have two Diplomacy tokens?
diplomacy.a5: You discard one at the end of the current Age. You keep the second one to use at the end of the next Age.
diplomacy.q6: If I use the Halicarnassus Wonder to build a Diplomacy card from the discard pile at the end of the game, what happens?
diplomacy.a6: You take the token, but since there are no more Military Conflicts, it serves no purpose.

blackCards.title: IV. Card Clarifications (Black Cards & Science)
blackCards.q1: How do the "Spy" cards (Green Mask symbol) work?
blackCards.a1: These cards allow you to copy one scientific symbol from a neighboring city.
blackCards.q2: Can a "Spy" card copy another "Spy" card?
blackCards.a2: No. You cannot copy a Mask symbol. You must copy a standard scientific symbol.
blackCards.q3: Do the "Black Market" and "Clandestine Dock" produce resources I can trade?
blackCards.a3: No. Resources produced by Black cards cannot be bought by neighbors.
blackCards.a4: It allows you to build Wonder stages for free (zero resource cost).
blackCards.a5: It gives coins immediately based on valid sets.

wonders.title: V. Wonders (Petra & Byzantium)
wonders.q1: How does the Al-Khazaneh of Petra work?
wonders.a1_li1: Side A: Grants Victory Points for all stages.
wonders.a1_li2: Side B: Stage 2 costs 14 Coins and grants 14 VP.
wonders.q2: How does the Cathedral of Byzantium work?
wonders.a2_li2: Side B: One stage grants a Diplomacy Token.

team.title: VI. Team Variant Rules
team.q1: How do we sit in a Team Game?
team.q2: Can I show my cards to my teammate?
team.q3: Can I trade resources with my partner?
team.a3: No special trading. You trade with your immediate neighbors as normal.
team.q4: How do Military Conflicts work in Teams?
team.a4: You do not fight your teammate. You only resolve combat against the opponent sitting on your other side.
team.a5_p1: Add the scores of the teammates together. Highest combined total wins.

setup.title: I. Setup & General Rules
setup.q1: How does the setup change when adding Armada?
setup.a1_p1: You must add Armada cards to the Age decks.
setup.a1_li1: Player Boards: Each player receives a Dockyard Board and 4 ship figures.
setup.q2: How many cards are dealt to each player?
setup.a2: At the start of each Age, players are dealt a hand of 8 cards.
setup.a3: Yes. You will take 7 turns per Age.
setup.q4: What happens to the 8th card in my hand?
setup.a4: It is discarded at the end of the Age.

navalConstruction.title: II. Naval Construction (Moving Ships)
navalConstruction.q1: How do I move a ship on my Dockyard board?
navalConstruction.a1: You can move a ship only when you construct a Building card. The card color dictates which ship moves (Red: Red Ship, etc). You must pay the Naval Construction cost.
navalConstruction.q2: Can I move a ship if I build a Brown, Grey, or Purple card?
navalConstruction.a2: No. Only Red, Yellow, Blue, and Green cards trigger Naval Construction.
navalConstruction.q3: Can I move a ship if I build a Wonder Stage?
navalConstruction.q4: Can I move a ship if I discard a card for 3 coins?
navalConstruction.q5: If I build a card for free, do I still have to pay to move the ship?
navalConstruction.q6: Can I use the resources produced by the card I am currently building to pay for the ship movement?
navalConstruction.q7: Can I trade with neighbors to pay for Naval Construction?

turnResolution.title: III. Turn Resolution & Timing
turnResolution.q1: In what order do I resolve my turn?
turnResolution.q2: Can I use the Halicarnassus Wonder ability to move a ship?

fleets.title: IV. The Fleets & Tracks
fleets.q1: What does the Red Fleet (Red Track) do?
fleets.a2: At the end of each Age, compare your Naval Strength against all other players.
fleets.q3: What does the Yellow Fleet (Yellow Track) do?
fleets.a3: It represents commercial power (Coins and Taxation).
fleets.q4: What does the Green Fleet (Green Track) do?
fleets.a4: It allows you to explore Islands.
fleets.q5: What does the Blue Fleet (Blue Track) do?

interactions.title: V. Gameplay Interactions
interactions.q2: If I discover an Island that gives resources, are those resources available to my neighbors?
interactions.q3: Does the "Lighthouse of Alexandria" Wonder effect apply to itself?
interactions.a4: Yes, but you need an extra Wonder board.
interactions.q5: What happens if I am Taxed but have no coins?
interactions.a5: If you have no coins, you simply lose nothing.

setup.title: I. Setup & Compatibility
setup.q1: Is the Edifice expansion compatible with the 1st Edition of the base game?
setup.a1: Yes. Unlike other expansions that had visual overhauls, Edifice was designed to be compatible with both the original 1st Edition and the newer 2nd Edition. The card backs and general aesthetics blend sufficiently with both versions.
setup.q2: How does setup change with Edifice?
setup.a2_p1: You do not shuffle Edifice cards into the Age decks. Instead:
setup.a2_li1: Edifice Decks: Separate the Edifice cards into three piles (Age I, II, and III).
setup.a2_li2: Selection: Randomly draw one Edifice card for each Age and place them in the center of the table, "Project" side up.
setup.a2_li3: Pawns: Place a specific number of Participation Pawns on the Edifice card based on the player count.
setup.a2_li4: Tokens: Place the new Debt tokens, Military tokens, and Coins in the supply.
setup.q3: Do I need to use the new Wonder boards (Ur and Carthage)?
setup.a3: No, they are optional. However, Ur is designed specifically for the Edifice expansion mechanics. Carthage can be used with just the base game if desired.

participation.title: II. Participation (Gameplay)
participation.q1: How do I participate in an Edifice project?
participation.a1_p1: You can only participate when you construct a Stage of your Wonder.
participation.a1_p2: When you build a Wonder stage, you may choose to also participate in the current Age's Edifice.
participation.a1_note: Note: You cannot participate when building a standard building or when discarding for coins.
participation.q2: What is the cost to participate?
participation.a2: You must pay the Cost of the Wonder Stage (resources shown on your board) PLUS the Participation Cost shown on the Edifice card (usually coins or resources). Both must be paid simultaneously.
participation.q3: Can I participate more than once in the same Edifice?
participation.a3_p1: Generally, No. You typically take one Participation Pawn per Age.
participation.a3_note: Exception: The Ur Wonder board has a special ability that allows its owner to participate in the same Edifice twice (acquiring two pawns and potentially earning the reward twice).
participation.q4: Can I participate if there are no Participation Pawns left on the card?
participation.a4: No. If the card is empty (meaning the project is completed), you cannot participate.
participation.a5: No. Participation is optional. However, if you do not participate and the project fails (is not completed), you will suffer a penalty.

completion.title: III. Completion, Rewards, & Penalties
completion.a1_p1: The Edifice is considered complete the moment the last Participation Pawn is removed from the card.
completion.a1_p2: Immediate Effect: The card is flipped to the "Constructed" side. Every player who has a Participation Pawn on their board immediately gains the Reward shown on the card.
completion.q2: What happens if the Edifice is NOT completed by the end of the Age?
completion.a2_p1: If there are still pawns on the Edifice card at the end of the Age, the project fails.
completion.a2_li1: Participants: Players who have a pawn gain nothing, but they suffer no penalty. Their pawns are returned to the box.
completion.a2_li2: Non-Participants: Players who do not have a pawn suffer the Penalty shown on the card.
completion.q3: When exactly do I get the Reward?
completion.a3_p1: You get the reward immediately when the project is finished (when the last pawn is taken). You do not wait until the end of the Age.
completion.a3_note: Strategic Note: This means you can use coin rewards from an Edifice to pay for costs later in the same Age.
completion.a4: Penalties often involve losing coins, discarding cards of a specific color, or losing military victory tokens.
completion.q5: What happens if I cannot pay the penalty (e.g., I have no coins)?
completion.a5_p1: If you cannot (or choose not to) pay the coin penalty, or if you cannot fulfill the card discard penalty, you must take a Debt Token.
completion.a5_li1: Age I Penalty: Take a -2 Point Debt Token.
completion.a5_li2: Age II Penalty: Take a -3 Point Debt Token.
completion.a5_li3: Age III Penalty: Take a -5 Point Debt Token.
completion.q6: Can I choose to take a Debt Token even if I have the coins?
completion.a6: The rules state that players who "cannot, or don't wish to" pay the loss take the Debt. This implies you may voluntarily take the Debt token to save your gold/cards, though it will cost you Victory Points at the end of the game.

wonders.title: IV. Wonder Specifics (Ur & Carthage)
wonders.a1_li1: Effect: Ur allows you to participate in the current Edifice project even if you have already participated once. You must still build a Wonder stage and pay the costs for each participation.
wonders.a1_li2: Benefit: If the Edifice is completed, you gain the Reward twice. If the Edifice fails, you still suffer no penalty (having at least one pawn protects you).
wonders.a2: Carthage provides a new way to build Wonder stages. It generally offers flexibility or specific structural advantages. While included in Edifice, it is fully compatible with the base game even if you aren't using the Edifice project cards.

scoring.title: V. Scoring & End Game
scoring.q1: Do Debt Tokens subtract from my final score?
scoring.a1_p1: Yes. At the end of the game, subtract the total value of your Debt tokens (-2, -3, or -5) from your final Victory Point total.
scoring.q2: Does the Edifice expansion add an extra turn or Age?
scoring.a2: No. You still play 3 Ages with 6 cards played per Age. The game length remains roughly the same.
scoring.q3: Do Participation Pawns grant Victory Points at the end of the game?
scoring.a3: No. The pawns themselves are just markers to indicate eligibility for Rewards or immunity from Penalties. They do not have an inherent VP value at the end of the game.
scoring.q4: If an Edifice gives me a Military Victory token as a reward, does it count for Military scoring?
scoring.a4: Yes. If you gain a Military Victory token (e.g., a "5 point" victory token) from an Edifice, add it to your military score at the end of the game. It is treated as if you won a conflict.

setup.title: I. Setup & Integration
setup.q1: How much money does each player start with?
setup.a1: Each player starts with 6 coins. (The Leaders expansion rules override the base game's 3 coins, and Cities does not change this further).
setup.q2: How do I prepare the Age decks with both expansions?
setup.a2_li1: Base Cards: Sort the standard Age cards by player count as normal.
setup.a2_li2: City (Black) Cards: Shuffle the City cards for each Age separately. Randomly draw a number of Black cards equal to the number of players for that Age and shuffle them into the corresponding Base Age deck.
setup.a2_li3: Guilds (Age III): Shuffle the Guilds from the Base Game, Leaders, and Cities together. Randomly select a number of Guilds equal to the number of players + 2. Shuffle these into the Age III deck.
setup.q3: How many cards are dealt to each player?
setup.a3_li1: Leaders Phase: 4 Leader cards (at the start of the game).
setup.a3_li2: Age Phases: 8 cards per player at the start of each Age (7 standard/Guild cards + 1 City card).
setup.q4: Which Wonder boards can I use?
setup.a4:
You can use all Wonder boards from the Base Game, the Colosseum of Rome (from Leaders), and Petra and Byzantium (from Cities).
Note: Rome is specifically designed to interact with Leader cards. Petra and Byzantium are designed to interact with City mechanics (Debt and Diplomacy).

leadersPhase.title: II. The Leaders Phase (Recruitment)
leadersPhase.q1: When do I play Leader cards?
leadersPhase.a1: At the very beginning of each Age (I, II, and III), before the Age cards are dealt, there is a Recruitment Phase. You play one Leader card during this phase.
leadersPhase.q2: Can I use the new City (Black) cards to pay for my Leaders?
leadersPhase.a2: No. The Recruitment Phase happens before you receive your hand of Age cards. You must pay for Leaders using the coins you currently have in your treasury.
leadersPhase.q3: What if I don't have enough money to recruit a Leader?
leadersPhase.a3_li2: Build a Wonder Stage using the Leader as a marker (paying the Wonder stage cost).
leadersPhase.q4: Does the "Rome" Wonder reduce the cost of all Leaders?
leadersPhase.a4: Yes. The Colosseum of Rome reduces the cost of recruiting Leaders by 2 coins.

gameplay.title: III. Gameplay Changes (Cities Mechanics)
gameplay.q1: How many turns do we play per Age?
gameplay.a1_li1: Since you start with 8 cards, you play 7 of them.
gameplay.a1_li2: The 8th (final) card is discarded at the end of the Age, and you do not get coins for it.
gameplay.a2_p1: Some Black cards or Wonder effects (like Petra Side B) force opponents to lose coins.
gameplay.a2_li1: If you have the coins, you must pay. You cannot voluntarily take Debt to save cash.
gameplay.a2_li2: If you cannot pay (you run out of coins), you take Debt tokens equal to the unpaid amount.
gameplay.a2_li3: Each -1 Debt token subtracts 1 Victory Point at the end of the game. (There are also -5 tokens).
gameplay.q3: How does Diplomacy (the Dove symbol) work?
gameplay.a3_p1: When you play a card with a Dove symbol (e.g., Residency, Spy Ring, or the Diplomat Leader), you take a Diplomacy Token and place it on your Wonder board.
gameplay.a3_li1: At the end of that Age, you are ignored during the Military Conflict phase.
gameplay.a3_li2: Your Left Neighbor and Right Neighbor fight each other instead of fighting you.
gameplay.a3_li3: You discard the token after using it. If you have multiple tokens, you only use one per Age; the others stay for future Ages.
gameplay.q4: What if I have a Diplomacy token but want to fight?
gameplay.a4: You cannot choose. If you have a token at the end of an Age, you must use it and sit out the conflict.

cards.title: IV. Card & Leader Clarifications
cards.q1: Can I combine the "Architect Cabinet" (City card) with Leaders?
cards.a1: Yes. The Architect Cabinet makes your Wonder stages free to build. This applies regardless of whether you use an Age card or a Leader card as the construction marker.
cards.q2: How does the "Solomon" Leader work with "Halicarnassus"?
cards.a2_p1: Both allow you to play from the discard pile.
cards.a2_li1: Solomon: Choose a card from the discard and play it for free.
cards.a2_li2: Halicarnassus: Look through the discard and play a card.
cards.a2_li3: Timing: If both effects happen in the same turn, Solomon resolves first.
cards.q3: Does the "Midas" Leader count coins I lost to Debt?
cards.a3: No. Midas scores 1 VP for every 3 coins currently in your treasury at the end of the game. Coins lost to taxes or spent are gone.
cards.q4: Does the "Bernice" Leader (from Cities) stack with "Nero" (from Leaders)?
cards.a4_li1: Nero: Gain 2 coins every time you gain a Victory token.
cards.a4_li2: Bernice: Gain 1 coin for every Victory token in your possession at the end of the game.
cards.a4_clarification: Clarification: During conflict resolution, these are separate triggers.
cards.q5: How do "Spy" cards (Green Mask) interact with Leader science symbols?
cards.a5_p1: Spy cards copy a science symbol from a neighbor's Green card. They generally cannot copy symbols from Leader cards unless the Leader card explicitly states it counts as a scientific structure.
cards.a5_p2: Rule of Thumb: Spies copy "Green Cards". Leaders are "White Cards". Therefore, Spies usually cannot copy symbols from Leaders.
cards.q6: Does the "Courtesan's Guild" copy a neighbor's Leader?
cards.a6:
Yes. When you build the Courtesan's Guild, you place a token on a neighbor's Leader card. You gain that Leader's effect.
Note: The neighbor also keeps the effect. You just copy it.
cards.q7: Does the "Gambling House" (City card) give coins for Leaders?
cards.a7: No. The Gambling House gives coins based on sets of Age cards. Leaders (White cards) are a separate category.

team.title: V. Team Play (2 vs 2 vs 2)
team.q1: Can we use Leaders in the Team Variant provided in Cities?
team.a1_li1: Teammates sit next to each other.
team.a1_li2: You can show your hand of cards (Age cards AND Leader cards) to your partner.
team.q2: How does the "Diplomacy" effect work in Team Play?
team.a2: If you use a Diplomacy token, you skip the conflict. Your teammate does not fight the person on your other side. The conflict resolution for your specific spot is simply skipped (0 points for you, and the opponent doesn't fight you).
team.q3: Do Leader effects that count "Neighbor's cards" include my teammate?
team.a3: Yes. Your teammate is one of your neighbors. If a Leader or Guild gives points for a neighbor's Blue cards, your teammate's Blue cards count.

scoring.title: VI. Scoring & End Game
scoring.q1: Do I count points for Black (City) cards separately?
scoring.a1: Yes. The scorepad provided in the Cities/Leaders expansions usually has a dedicated row (often marked with a black city icon) to sum points from Black cards.
scoring.q2: Do Leader points count as a separate category?
scoring.a2: Yes. There is a "Leader" row (symbolized by a white bust/head) on the scorepad.
scoring.a4: Most coins in the treasury wins.

setup.title: I. Setup & Integration
setup.q1: How much money does each player start with?
setup.a1: Each player begins the game with 6 coins. The Leaders expansion rules (which increase starting gold to 6) supersede the base game's 3 coins. The Armada expansion does not alter starting gold.
setup.q2: How do I prepare the decks?
setup.a2_li1: Leader Deck: Shuffle the Leader cards separately. Deal 4 to each player.
setup.a2_li2: Age Decks: Sort the Base Game cards by player count. Shuffle the Armada cards into the Base Game Age decks. For each Age, randomly draw a number of Armada cards equal to the number of players and shuffle them into that Age's deck.
setup.a2_li3: Guilds (Age III): Shuffle the Base Game Guilds and the Leaders Guilds together. Randomly select a number equal to the number of players + 2.
setup.q3: How many cards are dealt to each player per Age?
setup.a3: At the start of each Age (I, II, and III), players are dealt 8 cards (instead of the usual 7). This is because the deck now includes the Armada cards.
setup.q4: Which Wonder boards can I use?
setup.a4: You can use any Wonder from the Base Game, the Colosseum of Rome (from Leaders), and Siracusa (from Armada). Both expansion Wonders are compatible with this combination.
setup.a5: Yes. Every player receives a Dockyard board (placed vertically next to their Wonder) and 4 ship figures (Red, Yellow, Blue, Green) placed on the starting spaces.

leadersPhase.title: II. Gameplay Flow & The Leaders Phase
leadersPhase.q1: When do I play Leader cards?
leadersPhase.a1_p1: At the very beginning of each Age, before the Age cards are drafted, there is a Recruitment Phase.
leadersPhase.a1_li1: Look at your hand of Leaders (4 in Age I, 3 in Age II, 2 in Age III).
leadersPhase.a1_li2: Choose one to play (Recruit for coins, Build Wonder Stage, or Discard for 3 coins).
leadersPhase.a1_li3: The remaining Leaders stay in your hand for the next Age.
leadersPhase.q2: Does playing a Leader card trigger a ship movement (Naval Construction)?
leadersPhase.a2: No. Naval Construction allows you to move a ship only when you build a Red, Green, Yellow, or Blue card. Leader cards are considered White cards. Therefore, playing a Leader does not trigger the ability to move a ship on your Dockyard tracks.
leadersPhase.q3: Can I use the "Rome" Wonder ability to make Leaders cheaper?
leadersPhase.a3: Yes. The Colosseum of Rome reduces the cost of Leader cards by 2 coins.

gameplay.title: III. The Age Phase (Drafting & Naval Construction)
gameplay.q1: How many turns are in an Age?
gameplay.a1_li3: The 8th card is discarded at the end of the Age (you do not get coins for it).
gameplay.a2_p1: When you construct a Building card (Red, Green, Blue, or Yellow) from your hand:
gameplay.a2_li2: You may optionally pay the Naval Construction cost (shown on your Dockyard board) to move the ship of the matching color one space.
gameplay.q3: Can I move a ship if I build a Leader?
gameplay.q4: Can I move a ship if I build a Guild (Purple)?
gameplay.q5: Can I move a ship if I build a Wonder Stage?
gameplay.q6: Can I move a ship if I use the "Solomon" Leader?
gameplay.a6: No. Solomon allows you to pick a card from the discard pile and build it for free. The Armada rules explicitly state: "Resolve the construction of cards from the discard (no Naval Construction)." Therefore, you get the structure, but you cannot pay to move a ship.
gameplay.q7: Does the "Imhotep" Leader reduce the cost of Naval Construction?
gameplay.a7: No. Imhotep reduces the resource cost of constructing Wonder Stages. He does not affect Building cards or Naval Construction costs on the Dockyard.

interaction.title: IV. Interaction Specifics
interaction.a1_p1: When you advance your Yellow ship to a space with a Coin icon and a "loss" symbol for others:
interaction.a1_li2: All other players must lose the indicated amount of coins.
interaction.a1_li3: Important: Since you are not playing with the Cities expansion, there is no "Debt." If a player does not have enough coins to pay the tax, they lose all the coins they have, and the rest is ignored. They do not take negative points.
interaction.q2: How do Islands (Green Ship Track) work?
interaction.a2_p1: When your Green ship reaches an Island space, you draw cards from the Island deck (Level 1, 2, or 3). You keep one card.
interaction.a2_note: Note: Resources from Islands are generally for your personal use and cannot be traded to neighbors unless the card says otherwise.
interaction.q3: Does the "Tomyris" Leader apply to Naval Conflicts?
interaction.a3: No. Tomyris specifically refers to the Conflict Tokens (the -1 tokens from military defeats). In Armada, losing a Naval Conflict gives you a specific Naval Defeat Token. Tomyris typically applies to the standard Ground Conflict tokens.
interaction.q4: Do Leader science symbols count for "Scientific Strength"?
interaction.a4_p1:
No. Armada does not use "Scientific Strength." It uses "Naval Strength" (Red ships) and "Ground Strength" (Shields).
Clarification: Leader science symbols are added to your Green cards and Islands for the final science score.
interaction.q5: If I use "Bilkis" (buy any resource for 1 coin), can I use that resource to pay for Naval Construction?
interaction.a5: Yes. Bilkis allows you to buy a resource for 1 coin "once per turn." You can use this resource to satisfy the cost of the card or the cost of the Naval Construction.

scoring.title: V. Conflict & Scoring
scoring.q1: How do the two types of Conflict work?
scoring.a1_li1: Ground Conflict (Shields): Resolved at the end of the Age against your immediate neighbors. Uses Red Cards + Wonder Shields + Leader Shields.
scoring.a1_li2: Naval Conflict (Anchors): Resolved immediately after Ground Conflict. Compared against ALL players at the table. Uses Red Ship position + Armada cards with Anchors + Island cards with Anchors.
scoring.q2: Do Shields from Leaders (e.g., Hannibal, Caesar) count for Naval Conflict?
scoring.a2: No. Shields are for Ground Conflict. Anchors are for Naval Conflict.
scoring.q3: How do we calculate the final score?

setup.title: I. Setup & Integration
setup.q1: How much money does each player start with?
setup.a1: Each player begins the game with 6 coins. The Leaders expansion rules (which increase starting gold to 6) supersede the base game's 3 coins. The Edifice expansion does not alter starting gold.
setup.q2: How do I prepare the decks?
setup.a2_li1: Leader Deck: Shuffle the Leader cards separately. Deal 4 to each player.
setup.a2_li2: Age Decks: Use the standard Base Game Age cards. Edifice does not add cards to the Age decks.
setup.a2_li3: Edifice Cards: Separate by Age (I, II, III). Draw one random card for each Age and place them in the center, "Project" side up.
setup.a2_li4: Guilds (Age III): Shuffle the Base Game Guilds and the Leaders Guilds together. Randomly select a number equal to the number of players + 2.
setup.q3: How many cards are dealt to each player?
setup.a3_li1: Start of Game: 4 Leader cards (drafted to keep 4).
setup.a3_li2: Start of Each Age: 7 Age cards. (Edifice does not add cards to the hand).
setup.q4: Which Wonder boards can I use?
setup.a4: You can use any Wonder from the Base Game, the Colosseum of Rome (from Leaders), and Ur and Carthage (from Edifice).

recruitment.title: II. The Recruitment Phase (Leaders)
recruitment.q1: When do I play Leader cards?
recruitment.a1_p1: At the beginning of each Age (I, II, and III), there is a Recruitment Phase.
recruitment.a1_li1: Choose one Leader from your hand to play.
recruitment.a1_li2: Actions: Recruit (pay cost), Discard (gain 3 coins), or Build a Wonder Stage.
recruitment.q2: Can I participate in an Edifice project during the Recruitment Phase?
recruitment.a2_p1: Yes. This is the most important interaction between these two expansions.
recruitment.a2_li1: If you use your Leader card to Build a Stage of your Wonder during the Recruitment Phase, you may also pay the participation cost and place a pawn.
recruitment.a2_li2: Strategic Note: This allows you to participate in an Edifice before the Age even begins.
recruitment.q3: If I use the Leader "Imhotep," does he reduce the cost of the Edifice participation?
recruitment.a3: No. Imhotep reduces the resource cost of the Wonder Stage itself. He does not reduce the coins or resources required by the Edifice card.
recruitment.q4: Can I use the "Rome" Wonder ability to reduce the cost of Edifice participation?
recruitment.a4: No. The Colosseum of Rome reduces the cost of playing Leader cards. It does not affect the cost of Wonder stages or Edifice participation.

gameplay.title: III. Gameplay & Participation
gameplay.q1: When else can I participate in an Edifice?
gameplay.a1_p1: You can participate any time you construct a Stage of your Wonder. This can happen:
gameplay.a1_li1: During the Recruitment Phase (using a Leader card).
gameplay.a1_li2: During the standard Age Phase (using an Age card).
gameplay.q2: Can I participate more than once in the same Edifice?
gameplay.a2:
Generally, no.
Exception: If you are playing with the Ur Wonder, its specific power allows you to participate in the same project twice.
gameplay.q3: What happens if I participate in the Recruitment Phase and the Edifice is completed immediately?
gameplay.a3: If the Edifice is completed during the Recruitment Phase, the card is flipped, and rewards are gained immediately. You start the Age with those extra rewards.
gameplay.q4: If I use a Leader to build a Wonder stage, do I have to pay the Leader's coin cost?
gameplay.a4: No. When you use a card to build a Wonder stage, you ignore the card's own cost. You only pay the Wonder Stage cost (plus the Edifice cost if participating).

rewards.title: IV. Rewards & Penalties
rewards.q1: What happens if an Edifice project fails?
rewards.a1_li1: Participants: Get nothing, but suffer no penalty.
rewards.a1_li2: Non-Participants: Suffer the Penalty listed on the card.
rewards.q2: What if I cannot pay the penalty?
rewards.a2_p1: If you cannot pay the monetary penalty (or choose not to), you must take a Debt Token:
rewards.q3: Do Leaders protect me from penalties?
rewards.a3: Generally, no. However, Leaders that provide extra money or resources help you ensure you have the funds to either participate or pay the penalty.
rewards.q4: If an Edifice reward gives me 6 Coins, does that count for the "Midas" Leader?
rewards.a4: Yes. Midas grants +1 Victory Point for every 3 coins you have at the end of the game. Coins gained from an Edifice reward contribute to this.

scoring.title: V. End Game & Scoring
scoring.q1: How do I score the game?
scoring.q2: Do Leader science symbols count toward Edifice requirements?
scoring.a2: Edifices do not have "requirements" to build other than the cost. Science Leaders (like Aristotle) contribute to your science set scoring just like Green cards.

setup.title: I. Setup & Integration
setup.q1: How do I prepare the Age decks when playing with both expansions?
setup.a1_p1: You mix the Cities cards into the deck, but not the Edifice cards.
setup.a1_li1: Base Cards: Sort the standard Age cards by player count.
setup.a1_li2: City (Black) Cards: Shuffle the City cards for each Age separately. Randomly draw a number of Black cards equal to the number of players for that Age and shuffle them into the corresponding Base Age deck.
setup.a1_li3: Edifice Cards: These are not shuffled into the player decks. Draw one Edifice card for each Age (I, II, III).
setup.a1_li4: Guilds (Age III): Shuffle the Guilds from both the Base Game and Cities. Randomly select a number equal to the number of players + 2.
setup.q2: How many cards are dealt to each player?
setup.a2: Because you have added the Cities (Black) cards to the deck, each player receives a hand of 8 cards at the start of each Age (instead of the standard 7).
setup.a3: Yes. Since you are dealt 8 cards, you will take 7 turns per Age. You play one additional card per Age compared to the base game.
setup.a4_li2: Edifice: Participation Pawns, Military Victory tokens, and Edifice Debt tokens.
setup.a4_li3: Note: If your version of Cities included -1 Debt tokens, use those for Cities-specific penalties.
setup.q5: Can I use all the Wonder boards?
setup.a5: Yes. You can use the Base wonders, Petra and Byzantium (from Cities), and Ur and Carthage (from Edifice).

gameplay.title: II. Gameplay Flow & Participation
gameplay.q1: When can I participate in an Edifice project?
gameplay.a1_p1: You can only participate when you construct a Stage of your Wonder.
gameplay.a1_li1: You cannot participate when building a regular Black City card or a standard Building card.
gameplay.a1_li2: You cannot participate when discarding for coins.
gameplay.q2: How much does it cost to participate?
gameplay.a2: You must pay the Wonder Stage cost (shown on your board) PLUS the Participation cost (shown on the Edifice card).
gameplay.q3: Can I use the coins I gain from a City card to pay for Edifice participation in the same turn?
gameplay.a3: No. You must pay all costs before the card enters play. You cannot use resources/money from a card you haven't built yet.
gameplay.q4: What happens to the 8th card in my hand?
gameplay.a4:
It is discarded at the end of the Age. You do not get 3 coins for it.
Exception: If you have built Babylon (Side B, Stage 2), you can play this last card and still participate in the Edifice.

debts.title: III. Mechanics: Debt & Penalties
debts.q1: How does Debt work when combining these expansions?
debts.a1_p1: There are now two potential sources of Debt:
debts.a1_li1: Cities Debt: Some Black cards force opponents to lose coins. Unpaid losses result in taking -1 Debt Tokens.
debts.a1_li2: Edifice Debt: Failing an Edifice project penalty results in taking the specific Edifice Debt Token (-2, -3, or -5).
debts.q2: Can I voluntarily take Debt to save my coins?
debts.a2_li1: Cities: No. You must pay if you have the coins.
debts.a2_li2: Edifice: Yes. The rules allow players who "don't wish to" pay the penalty to take the Debt token.

diplomacy.title: IV. Mechanics: Diplomacy & Rewards
diplomacy.q1: Does Diplomacy protect me from Edifice penalties?
diplomacy.a1: No. Diplomacy (Dove) protects you only from Military Conflicts at the end of the Age. Being a diplomat does not exempt you from Edifice penalties.
diplomacy.q2: If an Edifice reward gives me a Military Victory Token, does Diplomacy prevent me from taking it?
diplomacy.a2: No. Diplomacy prevents you from participating in the conflict phase. Edifice rewards are a direct gain, not a combat resolution.
diplomacy.q3: Can I use the "Architect Cabinet" to participate in an Edifice?
diplomacy.a3: Yes, partially. The Architect Cabinet covers the resource cost of the Wonder stage, but you must still pay the Participation cost on the Edifice card yourself.

wonders.title: V. Wonder Specifics
wonders.q1: How does the "Ur" Wonder work with Cities?
wonders.a1: Ur allows you to participate in an Edifice project twice. Since Cities adds an extra turn, you have more opportunities to time your double participation.
wonders.a2: To participate in an Edifice while playing Petra Side B, you must pay the Coin cost for the Petra stage PLUS the coin cost for the Edifice. Ensure your economy is strong!
wonders.a3: Byzantium creates Diplomacy tokens, which is useful for avoiding the arms race and focusing resources on Edifice costs or expensive Blue/Black cards.

team.title: VI. Team Play (2 vs 2)
team.q1: Can we play the Team Variant with both expansions?
team.a1_p1: Yes. Both Cities and Edifice support team play. Participation in Edifices is still individual—if the project fails, only the partner who didn't participate takes the penalty.
team.a1_note: Note: You cannot pay for your partner's Edifice participation.
team.q2: How is the final score calculated?

setup.title: I. Setup & General Integration
setup.q1: How much money does each player start with?
setup.a1: Each player begins the game with 3 coins. Neither Armada nor Edifice changes the starting treasury value found in the base game.
setup.q2: How do I prepare the Age decks?
setup.a2_p1: You must integrate the Armada cards into the main deck, but not the Edifice cards.
setup.a2_li1: Base Cards: Sort the standard Age cards by player count as normal.
setup.a2_li2: Armada Cards: For each Age, randomly draw a number of Armada cards equal to the number of players and shuffle them into the corresponding Base Age deck.
setup.a2_li3: Edifice Cards: These are not shuffled into the decks. Separate them into three piles (Age I, II, III). Draw one random Edifice card for each Age and place them in the center.
setup.a2_li4: Guilds (Age III): Randomly select a number of Guilds equal to the number of players + 2 and shuffle them into the Age III deck.
setup.q3: How many cards are dealt to each player?
setup.a3: Because you have added the Armada cards to the deck, each player receives a hand of 8 cards at the start of each Age (instead of the standard 7).
setup.a4: Yes. Since you are dealt 8 cards, you will take 7 turns per Age. You play one additional card per Age compared to the base game.
setup.q5: What boards does each player need?
setup.a5: Each player receives one Wonder board and one Dockyard Board. The Dockyard board is placed vertically to the right of the Wonder board. You also place 4 ship figures (Red, Blue, Green, Yellow) on the starting spaces.

interaction.title: II. The "Golden Rule" of Interaction
interaction.q1: Can I move a Ship AND participate in an Edifice in the same turn?
interaction.a1_p1: No. This is the most critical rule interaction between these two expansions.
interaction.a1_li1: To participate in an Edifice, you must construct a Wonder Stage.
interaction.a1_li2: If you build a Wonder Stage, you must choose one option: Either pay to move a ship (Naval Construction) OR pay to participate in the Edifice.
interaction.a1_li3: You cannot do both in a single turn.

gameplay_naval.title: III. Gameplay: Naval Construction (Armada)
gameplay_naval.q1: How do I move a ship on my Dockyard board?
gameplay_naval.a1_p1: When you construct a Red, Green, Blue, or Yellow Building card, you may pay the Naval Construction cost shown on your Dockyard board to move the matching colored ship one space.
gameplay_naval.q2: Can I move a ship if I build a Brown, Grey, or Purple card?
gameplay_naval.a2: No. Only Red, Green, Blue, and Yellow cards trigger Naval Construction.
gameplay_naval.q3: Can I move a ship if I build a Wonder Stage?
gameplay_naval.a3:
Generally, No. Standard Wonder stages do not have a color, so they do not trigger Naval Construction.
(Note: If a specific Wonder treats stages as colored cards, you must still follow the Golden Rule).
gameplay_naval.q4: Can I use the resources produced by the card I am currently building to pay for the ship movement?
gameplay_naval.a4: No. You must pay all costs (the card's cost + the Naval Construction cost) simultaneously before the card enters play.
gameplay_naval.q5: Can I trade with neighbors to pay for Naval Construction?
gameplay_naval.a5: Yes. You may buy resources from your neighbors to pay for the ship's movement cost, following standard trading rules.

gameplay_edifice.title: IV. Gameplay: Edifice Projects
gameplay_edifice.q1: When can I participate in an Edifice project?
gameplay_edifice.a1: You can only participate when you construct a Stage of your Wonder. You cannot participate when building a regular Building card or discarding for coins.
gameplay_edifice.q2: What is the cost to participate?
gameplay_edifice.a2: You must pay the Wonder Stage cost (shown on your board) PLUS the Participation cost (shown on the Edifice card). If you pay both, you take a Participation Pawn.
gameplay_edifice.q3: What happens if the Edifice is completed?
gameplay_edifice.a3: As soon as the last Participation Pawn is removed from the card, the Edifice is constructed. Flip the card. Every player who has a pawn on their board immediately gains the reward.
gameplay_edifice.q4: What happens if the Edifice is NOT completed by the end of the Age?
gameplay_edifice.a4_p1: If there are still pawns on the Edifice card at the end of the Age, the project fails:
gameplay_edifice.a4_li1: Participants: Get nothing, but suffer no penalty. Pawns are returned to the box.
gameplay_edifice.a4_li2: Non-Participants: Suffer the Penalty listed on the card.
gameplay_edifice.q5: What happens if I cannot pay the Edifice penalty?
gameplay_edifice.a5_p1: If you cannot pay the penalty, you must take a Debt Token:
gameplay_edifice.a5_li1: Age I Penalty: -2 Point Debt Token
gameplay_edifice.a5_li2: Age II Penalty: -3 Point Debt Token
gameplay_edifice.a5_li3: Age III Penalty: -5 Point Debt Token

conflict_scoring.title: V. Conflict & Scoring
conflict_scoring.q1: How do the two types of Conflict work?
conflict_scoring.a1_li1: Ground Conflict: Resolved against immediate neighbors using Shields.
conflict_scoring.a1_li2: Naval Conflict: Resolved immediately after Ground Conflict against ALL players using Naval Strength (Anchors).
conflict_scoring.q2: Do Debt tokens subtract from my final score?
conflict_scoring.a2: Yes. At the end of the game, sum the value of all your Debt tokens and subtract that amount from your final score.
conflict_scoring.q3: What happens to the 8th card in my hand?
conflict_scoring.a3:
It is discarded at the end of the Age. You do not play it, and you do not gain 3 coins for it.
(Exception: Babylon Side B, Stage 2 allows you to play this card and potentially participate in an Edifice).
conflict_scoring.a4: When you advance your Yellow ship to a Tax space, other players must lose coins. Since Edifice provides Debt tokens, some players unify the rules by taking Debt for unpaid taxes, though standard Armada rules just enforce coin loss.
conflict_scoring.q5: Can I use the Halicarnassus Wonder to trigger Edifice participation or Naval Construction?
conflict_scoring.a5:
Naval Construction: No.
Edifice: No. Participation requires building a Wonder Stage.
conflict_scoring.q6: How do I score the game?

setup.title: I. Setup & Deck Building
setup.q1: How do I prepare the Age decks?
setup.a1_p1: You must integrate cards from both Cities and Armada into the Base Game deck:
setup.a1_li1: Base Cards: Sort the standard Age cards by player count.
setup.a1_li2: Cities (Black) Cards: For each Age, randomly draw a number of Black cards equal to the number of players and shuffle them into the deck.
setup.a1_li3: Armada Cards: For each Age, randomly draw a number of Armada cards equal to the number of players and shuffle them into the deck.
setup.a1_li4: Guilds (Age III): Mix the Guilds from Age III (Base + Cities). Randomly keep a number equal to the number of players + 2.
setup.q2: How many cards are dealt to each player?
setup.a2_p1: At the start of each Age, deal 9 cards to every player.
setup.a3: Yes. You will play 8 turns per Age. The 9th card is discarded at the end of the Age.

gameplay.title: II. Gameplay Interactions
gameplay.q1: Does building a City (Black) card trigger Naval Construction?
gameplay.a1:
Generally, No. Naval Construction is triggered by Red, Green, Blue, and Yellow cards. Most Black cards do not trigger ship movement.
Exception: If a Black card specifically has a colored mask icon (copying a color), check the specific wording. But standard Black cards do not move ships.
gameplay.q2: How does Debt (Cities) work with Taxes (Armada)?
gameplay.a2_p1: If a player triggers a Tax (Yellow Ship), all other players lose coins.
gameplay.a2_li1: If you have the coins, you must pay.
gameplay.a2_li2: If you cannot pay, you take Cities Debt Tokens (-1) equal to the unpaid amount.
gameplay.q3: Can I use the 'Diplomacy' token to avoid Naval Conflicts?
gameplay.a3: No. Diplomacy (Doves) protects you only from Ground Conflicts (Shields) against your neighbors. You must still participate in the Naval Conflict (Anchors) against all players.
gameplay.q4: Can I use the 'Architect Cabinet' to pay for ship movement?
gameplay.a4: No. The Architect Cabinet makes the Construction of the Card (or Wonder Stage) free. It does not cover the separate Naval Construction cost to move a ship.

wonders.title: III. Wonder Specifics
wonders.a1: Side B: Petra B requires huge amounts of coins to build Wonder stages. In Armada, coins are critical for Naval Construction. Managing your economy to support both the Wonder and your Fleet is the key challenge.
wonders.a2: Siracusa allows you to treat Wonder Stages as specific card colors. If you treat a stage as a 'Black Card' (if Siracusa allowed that), it wouldn't move a ship. However, Siracusa typically treats them as Base game colors (Red, Green, Yellow, Blue), allowing you to move ships while building your Wonder.

setup.q1: How much money does each player start with?
setup.a1: Each player begins the game with 3 coins. (Since the Leaders expansion is not being used, you revert to the base game's starting treasury).
setup.q2: How do I construct the Age decks?
setup.a2_p1: You must integrate cards from Cities and Armada into the main deck, while Edifice cards remain separate.
setup.a2_li1: Base Cards: Sort the standard Age cards by player count.
setup.a2_li2: Cities (Black) Cards: For each Age, randomly draw a number of Black cards equal to the number of players and shuffle them into the deck.
setup.a2_li3: Armada Cards: For each Age, randomly draw a number of Armada cards equal to the number of players and shuffle them into the deck.
setup.a2_li4: Edifice Cards: Place one per Age in the center (one per Age). Do not shuffle them into the decks.
setup.a2_li5: Guilds (Age III): Mix Guilds from Base and Cities. Randomly select a number equal to the number of players + 2.
setup.q3: How many cards are dealt to each player?
setup.a3_p1: You must deal 9 cards to each player at the start of every Age.
setup.q4: Which Wonder boards and components do I need?
setup.a4_li2: Player Boards: Each player needs their Wonder board plus a Dockyard Board and 4 ship figures.

gameplay.title: II. Gameplay Flow & Turns
gameplay.q1: How many turns are played per Age?
gameplay.a1_p1: You will play 8 turns per Age.
gameplay.a1_li3: The 9th card is discarded at the end of the Age.
gameplay.a1_li4: Exception: Babylon Side B, Stage 2 allows you to play the 9th card.

interaction.title: III. The "Golden Rule" of Interaction (Edifice + Armada)
interaction.q1: Can I move a Ship AND participate in an Edifice in the same turn?
interaction.a1_p2: If you are playing a Wonder like Siracusa where stages count as colored cards, or if any other effect allows you to trigger both, you must choose one. You cannot pay for both a Naval Construction and an Edifice Participation in the same turn.

mechanics.title: IV. Mechanics: Debt, Taxes, and Penalties
mechanics.q1: How do Taxes (Armada) interact with Debt (Cities)?
mechanics.a1_p1: When a player activates a Tax, all other players must lose coins.
mechanics.a1_p2: If a player cannot (or chooses not to) pay the tax, they must take Cities Debt tokens (-1 VP) equal to the unpaid amount.
mechanics.q2: Which Debt tokens do I use for Edifice penalties?
mechanics.a2: If an Edifice project fails, you suffer the Penalty. If monetary and you cannot pay, take the specific Edifice Debt Tokens (-2, -3, or -5). Do not take Cities (-1) tokens for Edifice penalties.
mechanics.q3: Can I voluntarily take Debt to save my coins?
mechanics.a3: Yes. For both Taxes and Edifice Penalties, rules imply a choice is allowed to take Debt instead of paying coins (though usually steeper).

diplomacy.title: V. Diplomacy (Cities) vs. Conflict (Armada)
diplomacy.q1: Does the "Diplomacy" (Dove) icon protect me from Naval Conflicts?
diplomacy.a1_li1: Diplomacy protects only from Ground Conflicts against immediate neighbors.
diplomacy.a1_li2: Naval Conflicts are global. You cannot withdraw from a Naval Conflict using a Diplomacy token.

specifics.title: VI. Specific Interactions
specifics.q1: Can I move a ship when building a Cities (Black) card?
specifics.a1: No. Naval Construction is triggered only by Red, Green, Blue, or Yellow cards.
specifics.q2: Can I move a ship if I use the "Architect Cabinet" (Cities)?
specifics.a2: No. The Architect Cabinet allows you to build Wonder Stages for free, which generally do not trigger Naval Construction.
specifics.q3: Do "Spy" cards (Cities) copy technology from Island cards (Armada)?
specifics.a3: No. Spy cards copy scientific symbols from neighbor's Green cards. Islands are not considered Green cards category-wise.

setup.q1: How much money does each player start with?

setup.q2: How many cards are dealt to each player at the start of an Age?
setup.a2_p1: You must deal 9 cards to each player.
setup.q3: How do I construct the Age decks?
setup.a3_p1: You must integrate cards from both expansions into the Base Game decks:
setup.a3_li1: Base Cards: Sort the standard Age cards by player count.
setup.a3_li2: Cities (Black) Cards: For each Age, randomly select a number of Black cards equal to the number of players and shuffle them into the Base deck.

setup.a3_li4: Guilds (Age III): Shuffle the Guilds from the Base Game, Leaders, and Cities. Randomly keep a number equal to the number of players + 2.
setup.q4: Which Wonder boards should I use?
setup.a4_p1: You can use any Wonder boards from the Base Game, Rome (from Leaders), Petra or Byzantium (from Cities), and Siracusa (from Armada). All are compatible.
setup.a4_note: Note: Remember to place a Dockyard Board to the right of every Wonder board and place the 4 ship figures on their starting spaces.

gameplay.title: II. Gameplay Flow & Turns
gameplay.q1: How many turns are played per Age?
gameplay.a1_p1: You will play 8 turns per Age.
gameplay.a1_li3: The 9th card is discarded at the end of the Age.
gameplay.a1_li4: Exception: Babylon Side B, Stage 2 allows you to play the 9th card.
gameplay.q2: When do I play Leader cards?
gameplay.a2: At the very beginning of each Age, before the hand of 9 Age cards is dealt, there is a Recruitment Phase. You play one Leader card during this phase.
gameplay.q3: Does playing a Leader card trigger a Naval Construction?
gameplay.a3: No. Leader cards are considered White cards. Naval Construction is only triggered by Red, Green, Blue, or Yellow cards.

naval.title: III. Naval Construction (Moving Ships)
naval.q1: Which cards allow me to move a ship?
naval.a1_p1: You may pay the cost to move a ship matching the color of the card you are building:
naval.q2: Can I move a ship when building City (Black), Leader (White), or Guild (Purple) cards?
naval.a2: No. Only Red, Green, Blue, and Yellow cards trigger Naval Construction.
naval.q3: Can I move a ship if I use Solomon or Halicarnassus?
naval.a3: No. The rules state that when you build a card from the discard pile (using Solomon or Halicarnassus), you cannot perform a Naval Construction.

mechanics.title: IV. Interaction of Mechanics: Debt, Taxes, and Diplomacy
mechanics.q1: How do Taxes (Armada) work with Debt (Cities)?
mechanics.a1_p1: When a player advances their Yellow ship to a "Tax" space, all other players must lose coins.
mechanics.a1_p2: If a player cannot (or chooses not to) pay the tax, they must take Debt tokens equal to the amount they could not pay (-1 VP each).
mechanics.q2: Does the "Diplomacy" (Dove) icon protect me from Naval Conflicts?
mechanics.a2_li1: Diplomacy protects you only from Ground Conflicts (Shields) against your immediate neighbors.
mechanics.a2_li2: Naval Conflicts are resolved against all players at the table. You cannot withdraw using a Diplomacy token.
mechanics.q3: Do "Ground Shields" count for Naval Strength?
mechanics.a3: No. Ground Shields (from Red, Black, or Leader cards) count only for Ground Conflict. Naval Strength comes solely from your Red Ship track, plus cards specifically showing an Anchor icon.

specifics.title: V. Card & Leader Specifics
specifics.q1: Can I use the "Architect Cabinet" to pay for Naval Construction?
specifics.a1: No. The Architect Cabinet makes the Wonder Stage cost free. It does not affect the cost of moving ships on your Dockyard.
specifics.q2: Does "Bernice" give coins for Naval Victories?
specifics.a2: Yes. Since winning a Naval Conflict awards a "Naval Victory Token," Bernice grants coins for these tokens just like Ground Victory tokens.
specifics.q3: Do "Spy" cards copy Science symbols from Islands?
specifics.a3: No. Spy cards copy a science symbol from a neighbor's Green card. Islands are not considered Green cards. Spies strictly target the Green structures in a neighbor's city.
specifics.q4: What is the Turn Resolution Order?
specifics.a4_li1: Pay Costs: Card, Wonder Stage, and Naval Construction.
specifics.a4_li4: Island Exploration: If Green ship reached an Island.
specifics.a4_li6: Coin Losses: Resolve Taxes and Coin Loss effects. Take Debt if unable to pay.

setup.title: I. Setup & Deck Preparation
setup.q1: How much money does each player start with?
setup.a1: Each player starts with 6 coins. Although the Base Game and Edifice standard is 3 coins, the Leaders expansion rules supersede this, increasing the starting treasury to 6.
setup.q2: How do I construct the Age decks?
setup.a2_li1: Base Cards: Sort the standard Age cards by player count.
setup.a2_li2: Cities (Black) Cards: Shuffle the Black cards separately. Randomly draw a number of Black cards equal to the number of players and shuffle them into the deck.
setup.a2_li3: Edifice Cards: These are not shuffled into the decks. Draw one Edifice card for each Age (I, II, III).
setup.a2_li4: Guilds (Age III): Shuffle the Guilds from the Base Game, Leaders, and Cities. Randomly keep a number equal to the number of players + 2.
setup.q3: How many cards are dealt to each player?
setup.a3_li2: Start of Each Age: 8 cards (7 Base + 1 City card).
setup.q4: Which Wonder boards can I use?
setup.a4: You can use all Wonder boards from the Base Game, Rome (from Leaders), Petra and Byzantium (from Cities), and Ur and Carthage (from Edifice).

gameplay.title: II. Gameplay Flow & Turn Structure
gameplay.q1: How many turns are played per Age?
gameplay.a1_li1: You receive a hand of 8 cards.
gameplay.a1_li3: The 8th card is discarded at the end of the Age (you do not get coins for it).
gameplay.q2: When do I play Leader cards?
gameplay.a2: At the start of each Age, before the Age cards are dealt, there is a Recruitment Phase. You play one Leader card during this phase (Recruit, Discard for 3 coins, or Build Wonder Stage).
gameplay.q3: Can I participate in an Edifice project during the Recruitment (Leader) Phase?
gameplay.a3_p2: If you use your Leader card to Build a Stage of your Wonder, you may also pay the Participation cost on the Edifice card to place a pawn on your board. This allows you to potentially secure a spot in the project before the Age officially begins.

edifice.title: III. Edifice Participation & Interaction
edifice.q1: When exactly can I participate in an Edifice?
edifice.a1_p1: You can participate only when you construct a Stage of your Wonder.
edifice.a1_note: Note: You cannot participate when building a standard structure (Black, Red, Blue, Green, Yellow, etc.).
edifice.q2: What costs do I pay to participate?
edifice.a2: You must pay the Wonder Stage cost (resources/coins shown on your board) PLUS the Participation cost (resources/coins shown on the Edifice card). Both must be paid at the same moment.
edifice.q3: Can I use the "Architect Cabinet" (City card) to pay for Edifice participation?
edifice.a3: No. The Architect Cabinet allows you to build the Wonder Stage for free. You must still pay the separate Participation cost to the bank if you wish to join the Edifice project.
edifice.q4: If an Edifice gives a Coin reward (e.g., 6 coins), does it count for the "Midas" Leader?
edifice.a4: Yes. Midas rewards you for total gold at the end of the game. If you earn coins from an Edifice reward and keep them until the end, they contribute to your Midas score (1 VP per 3 coins).

mechanics.title: IV. Diplomacy, Debt, and Penalties
mechanics.q1: How do the two types of Debt work?
mechanics.a1_p1: There are now two potential sources of Debt:
mechanics.a1_li1: Cities Debt: If a Black card forces you to lose coins and you cannot pay (usually -1 tokens).
mechanics.a1_li2: Edifice Debt: If an Edifice project fails and you did not participate, resulting in an Edifice Debt Token (-2, -3, or -5 points).
mechanics.a1_note: Scoring: At the end of the game, sum the negative value of all Debt tokens from both expansions.
mechanics.q2: Does having a Diplomacy (Dove) token protect me from Edifice penalties?
mechanics.a2: No. Diplomacy protects you only from Military Conflicts at the end of the Age. It has no effect on Edifice penalties.
mechanics.q3: If an Edifice reward gives me a Military Victory Token, does Diplomacy prevent me from taking it?
mechanics.a3: No. Diplomacy prevents you from fighting. It does not prevent you from receiving Military Victory tokens granted directly by rewards.

wonders.title: V. Specific Wonder Interactions
wonders.q1: How does the "Ur" Wonder (Edifice) work with Cities?
wonders.a1: Ur allows you to participate in an Edifice twice when building a specific Wonder stage. Since the Cities expansion adds an extra turn, you have more flexibility and time to gather the resources required for this expensive double-participation.
wonders.a2: To participate in an Edifice while playing Petra Side B, you must pay the Coin cost for the Wonder stage AND the cost for the Edifice. Money-generating Leaders or Cities cards are essential here.
wonders.q3: Can I use the "Rome" Wonder ability to reduce Edifice costs?
wonders.a3: No. Rome reduces the cost of playing Leader cards. It does not reduce the cost of Wonder stages or Edifice participation fees.

scoring.title: VI. End Game & Scoring
scoring.q1: How is the final score calculated?
scoring.q2: If I use "Imhotep" (Leader) to reduce my Wonder cost, can I still participate in an Edifice?
scoring.a2: Yes. Imhotep reduces the resource cost of the Wonder stage. You simply pay the reduced Wonder cost plus the standard Edifice participation cost.

setup.q1: How much money does each player start with?
setup.a1: Each player starts with 6 coins. While the base game and Edifice / Armada usually default to 3 coins, the Leaders expansion rules supersede this, increasing the starting treasury to 6.
setup.q2: How do I construct the Age decks?

setup.a2_li2: Armada Cards: For each Age, randomly draw a number of Armada cards equal to the number of players and shuffle them into the deck.
setup.a2_li3: Edifice Cards: Placed in the center of the table (one per Age). Do not shuffle them into the decks.

setup.a3_li2: Start of Each Age: 8 cards (7 Base + 1 Armada card).
setup.q4: Which Wonder boards can I use?
setup.a4:
You can use all Wonder boards from the Base Game, Rome (from Leaders), Siracusa (from Armada), and Ur and Carthage (from Edifice).
Note: Remember to place a Dockyard Board to the right of every Wonder board.

gameplay.title: II. Gameplay Flow & Turn Structure
gameplay.q1: How many turns are played per Age?
gameplay.a1_li1: You receive a hand of 8 cards.
gameplay.a1_li3: The 8th card is discarded at the end of the Age.
gameplay.q2: What is the sequence of play for an Age?
gameplay.a2_li2: Age Phase: Play 7 turns using the Age cards.
gameplay.a2_li3: Conflict Resolution: Ground Conflicts and Naval Conflicts.

golden_rule.title: III. The "Golden Rule" of Interaction (Edifice + Armada)
golden_rule.q1: Can I move a Ship AND participate in an Edifice in the same turn?
golden_rule.a1_p1: No. This is the most critical interaction rule.
golden_rule.a1_li1: To participate in an Edifice, you must construct a Wonder Stage.
golden_rule.a1_li2: If you construct a stage of your Wonder, you must choose one: Either pay to Participate in the Edifice OR pay to perform a Naval Construction.
golden_rule.a1_li3: You cannot do both actions in a single turn.

leaders_phase.title: IV. Leaders Phase Interactions
leaders_phase.q1: Can I participate in an Edifice during the Recruitment (Leaders) Phase?
leaders_phase.a1: Yes. If you choose to use your Leader card to Build a Stage of your Wonder, you are constructing a Wonder stage and may pay the Participation cost and place a pawn.
leaders_phase.q2: Can I perform a Naval Construction during the Leaders Phase?
leaders_phase.a2: Generally, No. Naval Construction is triggered by building Red, Green, Blue, or Yellow cards. Leaders are White cards. Even if building a Wonder stage, standard stages do not have a color.
leaders_phase.q3: Does the "Rome" Wonder reduce the cost of Edifice participation?
leaders_phase.a3: No. Rome reduces the cost of playing Leader cards. It does not affect Wonder stages, Naval Construction, or Edifice participation fees.

armada_mechanics.title: V. Armada Mechanics (Naval Construction)
armada_mechanics.q1: Which cards allow me to move a ship?
armada_mechanics.a1: You may pay to move a ship only when you build a card of a matching color: Red (Military), Yellow (Commercial), Blue (VP), or Green (Islands).
armada_mechanics.q2: Can I move a ship if I use the Solomon Leader?
armada_mechanics.a2: No. The Armada rules state that when building from the discard pile (via Solomon or Halicarnassus), you cannot perform a Naval Construction.
armada_mechanics.q3: How do Taxes work with Edifice Debt tokens?
armada_mechanics.a3: In this combination, if you cannot pay an Armada Tax, you simply lose all your coins. You generally do not take Edifice Debt tokens for unpaid Armada taxes; those are reserved for Edifice penalties.

edifice_mechanics.title: VI. Edifice Mechanics (Projects)
edifice_mechanics.q1: What happens if an Edifice project fails?
edifice_mechanics.a1_li1: Participants: Get nothing, but suffer no penalty.
edifice_mechanics.a1_li2: Non-Participants: Suffer the Penalty (discard cards, lose VP, lose coins).
edifice_mechanics.q2: What if I cannot pay the Edifice penalty?
edifice_mechanics.a2_p1: If you cannot pay the monetary penalty, you must take an Edifice Debt Token:
edifice_mechanics.q3: If an Edifice reward gives me Coins, do they count for the "Midas" Leader?
edifice_mechanics.a3: Yes. Midas scores based on your total treasury at the end of the game. Coins earned from Edifices contribute to this total.

wonders.title: VII. Specific Wonder Interactions
wonders.q1: How does "Siracusa" (Armada Wonder) work with Edifice?
wonders.a1: Siracusa allows you to treat Wonder Stages as cards of a specific color. However, you must still follow the Golden Rule: Choose either to move the ship or participate in the Edifice. You cannot pay for both.
wonders.q2: How does "Ur" (Edifice Wonder) work with Leaders?
wonders.a2: Ur allows you to participate in an Edifice twice. You can trigger this during the Leaders Phase if you use a Leader card to build that specific Wonder stage.
wonders.q3: Can I use "Imhotep" (Leader) to facilitate Edifice participation?
wonders.a3: Yes. Imhotep reduces the resource cost of building the Wonder Stage, making it easier to afford the separate Participation Cost.
